images.alt: The visual is a vibrant, comic book-style illustration inspired by Franco-Belgian aesthetics, featuring bold black outlines and flat, saturated colors. The setting is a surreal, tropical beach with impossibly bright yellow sand and a turquoise ocean. I am standing casually, leaning my shoulder against the trunk of a massive, curvy coconut palm. I’m wearing a loud, floral Hawaiian shirt unbuttoned over a tank top, paired with board shorts. In one hand, I’m holding a coconut with a straw stuck in it, and in the other, I’m lazily holding a whole lime. My expression is a mix of a smirk and a side-eye, looking off to the side like I’m plotting something. The shading is simple and graphic, using solid blocks of color. The vibe is kitschy and laid-back—a perfect snapshot of tropical absurdity.

The visual is a vibrant, comic book-style illustration inspired by Franco-Belgian aesthetics. The setting is a surreal, oversized tropical beach with impossibly bright yellow sand and a turquoise ocean that looks like solid gelatin. I am lying on a tiny strip of sand, wearing a loud, floral Hawaiian shirt and oversized sunglasses, looking completely frazzled. Next to me is a massive, exaggerated coconut tree, and I am holding a whole coconut with a straw sticking out of it, spilling liquid everywhere. My expression is a mix of panic and confusion, eyes wide and staring. The art style features bold, uniform black outlines and flat, saturated colors without gradients. The background includes a cartoonish sun wearing sunglasses and a crab that looks suspiciously angry. The vibe is manic and colorful—a perfect snapshot of lyrical madness.

Geek Pride Day

images.alt:
Geek Pride Day is celebrated on May 25th to honor geek culture and embrace the positive aspects of being a geek. The holiday was first established in Spain in 2006 and has since spread to other countries around the world.
On Geek Pride Day, people celebrate by indulging in their geeky hobbies and interests, such as reading comics, playing video games, watching sci-fi movies, and participating in cosplay. The holiday also encourages people to embrace their individuality and take pride in their unique passions and talents.
Geek Pride Day is often associated with science fiction and fantasy culture, as well as technology and gaming. It is a day for geeks of all stripes to come together and celebrate their shared interests and enthusiasms, and to recognize the valuable contributions that geeks have made to society in fields such as science, technology, and entertainment.

images.alt: The visual is a stark, high-contrast black and white portrait that radiates intimidating power and ethereal presence. The setting is a void-like, endless white space, isolating the subject completely. I am standing tall, wearing a simple, flowing floor-length dress that catches an invisible breeze, creating dramatic, sweeping folds around my legs. Despite the softness of the dress, my posture is rigid and unyielding. My hands are clasped loosely in front of me. My face is the focal point—dead center, filling the frame. My expression is a solid, unshakeable glare, eyes narrowed slightly, looking directly through the camera lens with an intensity that feels heavy. The lighting is flat and unforgiving, exposing every texture of skin and fabric without mercy. The vibe is commanding and transcendent—"I am here, I am vast, and I am not an object for your consumption."

Ceremony:
Captain Freeman: I now pronounce you, h…
*Wednesday glares…
Captain Freeman: Uhhh… married. You may now k…
Wednesday: Don’t say it.
Boimler: Oh please?
*Wednesday glares…
Boimler: But we promised! “Until death do we part.”
Wednesday: I never said “we”.
Boimler: Wait,… you…?
*Crewman checks drone video, showing Wednesday saying “until death do you part.”
Boimler: Okay, fine. But please? I promise to be good for the rest of my life!
Wednesday: How long is that?
Boimler: Ummm… at least a while longer?
Wednesday: (narrows her eyes) Are you sure?
Boimler: *gulps* …yes?
Wednesday: *sighs* Very well… I will allow it… *this* time.
*Boimler kisses Wednesday *very* briefly.
Wednesday: That was not… unpleasant. You may live.
*Boimler sighs with relief. Wednesday dismisses Mariner, who gives her a Klingon salute and steps back.

*Moopsy finds the bone-in wings, “Moopsy!”, and goes to town on them.

Wednesday: (Watching with a deadpan stare) "You're ruining his appetizer, Boimler. He prefers the crunch."
